response.justification: Bogdan is a strong full-stack and distributed systems engineer with genuine geospatial exposure through GISCollective and Green Map, but the role's core requirement is modern C++ or Rust for a routing data pipeline, and his C++ experience is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor's thesis project — neither recent nor deep. His current and recent work is squarely in TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, with no Rust experience mentioned, and no exposure to navigation routing, map tile generation, or the kind of high-throughput geospatial data pipelines this role demands.
strengths: Geospatial domain familiarity through GISCollective and Green Map GIS platform (event-driven, Kubernetes-deployable, production-used)
Distributed systems and high-throughput service experience at Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests/second with low-latency DLang apps
Strong engineering discipline demonstrated through TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and craftsmanship practices across multiple roles
concerns: No modern C++ or Rust proficiency — C++ last used meaningfully in 2014–2015, absent from skills list entirely
No experience with routing pipelines, navigation tile generation, or map data transformation workflows
Primary stack (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) is orthogonal to the role's technology requirements; retraining cost is high for a mid-senior IC position
